common.footer: © 2025 by Jason Zhu | Senior Software Engineering Leader

hero.subtitle: Technology Management | Software Engineering | Team & Platform Builder
hero.intro: Sr. technology leader with 25+ years of experience leading software architecture, development, and transformation initiatives in Health, Insurance, and Group Benefits. Expert in AI-powered automation, scalable enterprise platforms, cloud and agile delivery. Known for driving innovation, operational efficiency, and measurable business outcomes.

about.title: Jason Zhu
about.subtitle: About Me
aboutParagraphs: I’m a technology leader with 25+ years of experience building and scaling enterprise software platforms across healthcare, insurance, and group benefits. My work sits at the intersection of technology strategy, software engineering, and people leadership. I’ve led large-scale modernization initiatives—cloud migration, legacy re-architecture, and API-driven platforms—while delivering highly reliable systems that process millions of transactions annually. Whether defining a long-term technology roadmap or guiding teams through complex delivery challenges, I focus on creating solutions that are scalable, resilient, and closely aligned with business outcomes.

I’m deeply passionate about building strong engineering teams and healthy delivery cultures. Over the years, I’ve grown and led cross-functional teams of 50+ engineers, improving delivery speed, quality, and engagement through Agile practices, DevOps, and Team Topologies. I remain hands-on by choice—still coding, prototyping, and experimenting—especially in areas like AI-assisted development and automation. I believe that great technology leadership is about multiplying impact: empowering people, simplifying complexity, and turning innovation into measurable value.
funFacts: Sports Enthusiast: Whether it's the finesse of badminton, the precision of snooker, or the thrill of soccer (especially a last-minute goal!), I’m always game for competition and strategy.
Travel Buff: I make the most of my vacations by exploring new cultures, landscapes, and cuisines. Each trip adds a new layer to how I see the world — and sometimes inspires my next tech experiment.

items.title: TELUS AdjudiCare

cards.title: About
bodyLines: A health insurance claims adjudication and payment solution hosted in aws with the world class level of resilience, scalability, and security.
Robust configuration rules enable TPAs and insurers to customize claims processing and payment workflows efficiently.
API driven architecture allows seamless integration with third-party systems for eligibility and claim data exchange.

cards.title: Claim Journey
bodyLines: <Icon icon='arrowRightToBracket' /> Claim received electronically or batch imported by users
<Icon icon='personCircleQuestion' /> Claim pended for manual review
<Icon icon='gears' /> Claim adjudicated based on plan design and configuration rules
<Icon icon='moneyCheckDollar' /> Payment processed automatically via EFT or cheque generation

cards.title: Impact
bodyLines: <Icon icon='peopleLine' /> Hundreds of thousands certificates onboarded with ease and equipped with mobile access
<Icon icon='hospitalUser' /> Registered healthcare providers across Canada enabled for electronic claims submission
<Icon icon='handHoldingDollar' /> Significant operational and benefit cost savings to insurers, TPAs and plan sponsors through automation and fraud detection.

hero.title: Cherish Integrated Services
hero.subtitle: Designing a Charity Event Website and Donation Platform

organization.heading: Organization Background
body: Cherish Integrated Services (CHIS) is a non-profit organization and registered charity that serves individuals with developmental disabilities in the Greater Toronto Area. They offer a variety of programs for each age group to aid in vocational training, job venture, and or daily living.

CHIS hosts an annual Love Gala to raise funds to further expand their services and facilities. This year, proceeds will go towards developping their new Vocational Training Centre. This new facility will help more developped trainees work in a real cafe environemnt and learn employable skills.

role.heading: My Role
role.title: Digital Marketing Coordinator
body: As the sole designer during my term, I developped an website for the 2024 Cherish Love Gala charity event using the platform Raisley. I ensured to include important details regarding the event and managed incoming donations and ticket sales.
In addition to the design aspect, I heavily participated in developping the sponsorship package and communicating with local businesses and media outlets in regards to the event.

leftItems.title: Performer Profiles
leftItems.description: Creating an introductory and donation page for each performer where supporters can pledge their donations to.

leftItems.title: Ticket Sales & Donations
leftItems.description: Linking Powr.io for ticket sales and food cost deductions in tax receipts.

rightItems.title: Event Details
rightItems.description: Informing guests on the location, date, time, and ticket inclusions. Describing the cause in which all proceeds will go towards.

rightItems.title: Sponsors
rightItems.description: List all supporters, event sponsors, and prize (raffle and silent auction) sponsors in the footer with linkbacks to their website.

rightImage.alt: What is Cherish Love Gala page screenshot

hero.title: SimonSaves
hero.subtitle: Rotman Commerce Innovation Group x Rotman Commerce Fintech Association Design Dash Finalist

overview.heading: Overview
body: Our group of 4 placed in the Top 3 for a case competition that challenged us to design a solution to solve an issue in the financial technology sector. Our solution introduced an app that uses machine learning to recommend recipes and spending on meals based on users' budgets and dietary restrictions and preferences.

keyFeatures.title: Key Features
items: Filtered recipe and grocery search based on dietary restrictions and recommendations
Financial food budgeting summary on homepage
Information Cards providing tips and facts on different ingredients
Online community to chat with other app users and ask questions
Scrolling advertisement banners on homepage to promote ongoing discounts and sales

role.heading: My Role
role.title: UX Designer and Logo Creator
body: Due to my experience using Figma through Google's online UX Design course, with another team member, we took on the task of developing a user friendly app for financial budgeting and food education. The diverse skills of our team enabled me to lead the design process and guide my team mate through the basic functions of Figma.

Together, we worked on paper wireframes to brainstorm ideas, followed by creating mockups for each page on the app. I then took on the sole responsibility of the prototype stage, ensuring seamless interactions for all buttons.

Because of my extensive visual arts background, I also took on the task of creating a personalized mascot and logo for our brand. Our intended brand image was educational and friendly, thus leading to the creation of Simon the Persimmon. As a group, we decided on the name as we believed that consumers would immediately associate the unique fruit to our brand because of its rhyming name and uncommon use.

results.heading: Results
body: Our group made it to the final presentation round where we got the opportunity to pitch our idea to a panel of experienced UX and Product designers. The final presentation included 10 minutes for pitch delivery and 5 minutes for panelists' questions.

learnings.title: Learnings
items: The Design Process is a continual cycle
Background knowledge in technology is crucial
Utilize diverse team member skills with task delegation, but ensure all members have a say in each aspect of the project to maximize potential
